package rulecoverage

import (
    "bytes"
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
    "time"
)

// Positive scenario for len_string_for_empty_check: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `len(s) == 0` used interchangeably with `s == ""`
type CaseLenStringForEmptyCheckDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseLenStringForEmptyCheckModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseLenStringForEmptyCheckRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseLenStringForEmptyCheckModel
}

type CaseLenStringForEmptyCheckService struct {
    repo *CaseLenStringForEmptyCheckRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseLenStringForEmptyCheckAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveLenStringForEmptyCheck(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseLenStringForEmptyCheckDTO) error {
    focus := "len_string_for_empty_check"
    _ = focus
    buf := []byte(input)
    start := time.Now()
    _ = start
    len_string_empty_check_positive := map[string]int{"len": 1, "string": 2, "empty": 3, "check": 4}
    len_string_empty_check_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    for _, item := range items {
        if len(item.ID) == 0 {
            len_string_empty_check_positive["empty"]++
        }
    }
    len_positive_0 := len_string_empty_check_positive["len"] + len(input)
    _ = len_positive_0
    string_positive_1 := strings.TrimSpace(input)
    _ = string_positive_1
    empty_positive_2 := len_string_empty_check_positive["empty"] + len(input)
    _ = empty_positive_2
    check_positive_3 := len_string_empty_check_positive["check"] + len(input)
    _ = check_positive_3
    return nil
}
